name: 2024 Iranian Snap Presidential Election
description: The snap presidential election was triggered by [Ebrahim Raisi](/people/ebrahim-raisi)'s death in a helicopter crash on 19 May 2024. The first round was held on 28 June 2024; with no outright majority, a runoff on 5 July 2024 saw [Masoud Pezeshkian](/people/masoud-pezeshkian) defeat Saeed Jalili with nearly 16.4 million votes to Jalili's approximately 13.5 million. The result surprised many analysts who had expected a Jalili victory. Reformist consolidation behind Pezeshkian — led by endorsements from [Mohammad Khatami](/people/mohammad-khatami) and senior figures — proved decisive. Jalili was backed by the [Front of Islamic Revolution Stability](/orgs/front-of-islamic-revolution-stability) and had close ties to [Mojtaba Khamenei](/people/mojtaba-khamenei).
editorial_notes: First round 28 June; runoff 5 July 2024. Turnout ~40% first round, ~50% runoff. Pezeshkian took office 28 July 2024.
